behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is between changed-file discovery and analysis scheduling. A unit test of just the filter logic can miss wiring bugs where the scanner still receives unchanged files.
behaviors.test_description: Create a local repo fixture with a base branch and PR branch where only a subset of files differ. Run the pipeline step end-to-end at module level (changed-file collector + analysis dispatcher) and assert only changed paths are handed to the linter; verify unchanged files are never enqueued.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: The behavior spans multiple modules: linter output parsing, severity bucketing, and comment rendering/posting payload creation. A pure unit test on grouping alone may miss schema/adapter mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: Feed mixed-severity linter findings through the real parser + grouping + comment-builder path, with a fake PR-comment sink capturing the final body. Assert the emitted comment contains distinct error/warning/info sections with correct findings under each.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: This is a runtime/performance boundary tied to process execution, filesystem I/O, and CI-like execution constraints. Mocks can hide real latency and scheduling overhead.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual lint step as a subprocess against a generated 200-file fixture repository in CI (or CI-like runner), measure wall-clock runtime, and assert completion under 10 minutes including report generation.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: The core failure is subprocess crash handling and continuation semantics. Simulated exceptions can miss real crash modes (non-zero exit, signal termination, partial output).
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixture where one file triggers an actual linter subprocess crash. Execute the pipeline step with real subprocess invocation; assert remaining files are analyzed, partial failures are recorded, and a PR-comment payload is still produced/post attempted.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily deterministic dedup logic on finding keys (file/line/rule/message). No I/O or external boundary is required to surface the real defect.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup function with synthetic findings containing intentional same-line overlaps from different rule emitters and exact duplicates; assert output keeps one canonical entry per dedup key while preserving distinct non-duplicates.
